rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/26/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies of the petition and proof of service have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) A quick skim of the pleading online shows that the accounting period exceeds two years. [LRSF 14.34 A.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any new papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/16/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. The probate referee WAS APPOINTED on 3/10/20. Examiner has received a copy of the objections. Only one courtesy copy of each filed paper needs to be provided.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALFRED JOSHUA DIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/8/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Despite what is alleged in the "answer sheet" that was captioned as the Third Supplement to the petition, courtesy copies of the petition, the first two supplements, and the proof of service have not yet been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Only a proposed order was received. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Proof of service of notice of the hearing on 1/8/24 with the remote appearance instructions attached must be filed. (3) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] Fractions are not sufficient. (4) If the attorney's declaration in support of the request for fees for extraordinary services did not fully comply with CRC 7.702 and did not support fees billed by a paralegal, it must be supplemented with such information. (Fees requested for time billed by a paralegal must be supported by the attorney's declaration regarding the paralegal's compliance with Business and Professions Code Sect. 6450.) Deadline is 12/27/23 for delivering the endorsed filed courtesy copies of the petition and subsequently filed papers to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUN WAH MOON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/14/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A post-deceased heir's successor-in-interest is NOT necessarily the deceased heir's issue. Did Frank Toy Moon and Linda Sue Moon die intestate? Did neither have a will that is being probated or an administrator appointed by a court to administer his/her estate or a trust that is being administered by a trustee? (2) Who are the individuals who were given notice and waived bond but were not otherwise mentioned in any verified pleading? (3) Not all interested parties have waived bond. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues by 2/2/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Do not "respond" to issues as though the issues were a test. A supplement or amendment to a petition must stand on its own and just provide the missing or different information. A correct proof of service should simply be filed; narrating that one will be filed is meaningless.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SANDRA GRIFFIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: *REPOSTED 12/11/23 at 8:00 am* Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues have not been cleared: (1) Unless Petition #8 is supplemented to set forth heirs at law, need a declaration of due diligent search of the decedent's heirs at law. Petition merely states that the decedent was not survived by no blood relatives. (2) Proposed Order must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A. (3) If surviving spouse is the only successor in interest, if administration is needed. (4) Notice of Petition to Administer to William Brotherton. Notice of instruction of remote appearance to be served as well. (5) Notice of Petition to Administer with a copy of petition to Scott J. Judson who filed RSN. Notice of instruction of remote appearance to be served as well. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KERN YEE WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/17/2024. The following issues are to be cleared two weeks prior: (1) Need Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [JC Form DE-147S] to be filed. See the Local Rule of SF 14.18.E. (2) Petition caption and #2.b to set forth the proposed personal representative as Administrator with Will Annexed, not Executor. Prob. Code sec.8440. (3) Petition #8 to identify the ancestor(s) of each second generation listed. (4) Need to re-notice and re-publish to mirror that Petition offers a will to be admitted. Notice #4 is silent; thus, the publication does not indicate the information. (5) Notice to include Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access to all persons who are entitled to notice. LR 4.1.C.(6). (6) Proposed Order to be submitted before the hearing. LRSF 14.7.A. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BARBARA L. MARTTILA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/29/24.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GARY H. SUEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate is set for 12/30/24. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 12/30/24 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FILOMENA A DALOPE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will. No appearance is required. Status on close of the estate is set for 01/13/2025.
